title: vicia narbonensis subsp. faboidea
intro: Vicia narbonensis subsp. faboidea, also known as the broad bean or faba bean, is a sub-species of the vetch plant, which is a type of legume native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, due to its high fiber and protein content. The plant is also known for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, thanks to its rich antioxidant and polyphenol content. In herbal preparations, the seeds of Vicia narbonensis subsp. faboidea are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that support digestive health and overall well-being.
